records.ety: [From a French type founder named Bourgeois, or fr. F. bourgeois of the middle class; hence applied to an intermediate size of type between brevier and long primer: cf. G. bourgeois, borgis. Cf. Burgess.]

defs: A size of type between long primer and brevier. See Type.
notes: ☞ This line is printed in bourgeois type.

records.ety: [F., fr. bourg town; of German origin. See Burgess.]

defs: A man of middle rank in society; one of the shopkeeping class.
Characteristic of the middle class, as in France.
